ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network session */,
  "start_timestamp" TEXT /* Start timestamp of the session */,
  "end_timestamp" TEXT /* End timestamp of the session */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with the session */,
  "provider" TEXT /* Telecommunications provider (e.g., TMobile) */,
  "radio_type_used" TEXT /* Type of radio technology used (e.g., NSA+LTE) */,
  "direction" TEXT /* Direction of the session (e.g., uplink, downlink) */,
  "iperf_type" TEXT /* Type of Iperf test (e.g., parallel-merged) */,
  "run_number" INTEGER /* Run number of the session */,
  "seq_no" INTEGER /* Sequence number of the session */,
  "time_since_start" INTEGER /* Time since the start of the session */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the session took place */,
  "network_tower_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network tower involved in the session */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., active, completed, failed) */,
  "session_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_sessions_provider FOREIGN KEY ("provider") REFERENCES providers ("provider_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_sessions_radio_type_used FOREIGN KEY ("radio_type_used") REFERENCES radio_types ("radio_type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_sessions_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_sessions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated network session */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data entry */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the device's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the device's location */,
  "moving_speed" REAL /* Speed of the device in meters per second */,
  "compass_direction" INTEGER /* Compass direction of the device */,
  "nr_status" TEXT /* Network status (e.g., CONNECTED) */,
  "rsrp" INTEGER /* Reference signal received power (RSRP) */,
  "nr_ss_rsrp" INTEGER /* NR SS RSRP */,
  "nr_ss_sinr" INTEGER /* NR SS SINR */,
  "rsrp_avg" INTEGER /* Average RSRP */,
  "nr_ss_rsrp_avg" INTEGER /* Average NR SS RSRP */,
  "nr_ss_sinr_avg" INTEGER /* Average NR SS SINR */,
  "mobile_rx" INTEGER /* Mobile receive data (bytes) */,
  "mobile_tx" INTEGER /* Mobile transmit data (bytes) */,
  "current_now" REAL /* Current now (mA) */,
  "voltage_now" REAL /* Voltage now (mV) */,
  "nr_status_array" TEXT /* Array of network status */,
  "downlink_mbps" REAL /* Downlink throughput (Mbps) */,
  "uplink_mbps" REAL /* Uplink throughput (Mbps) */,
  "sw_power" REAL /* Software power (mW) */,
  "sw_power_rolled" REAL /* Rolled software power (mW) */,
  "sw_power_baseline" REAL /* Baseline software power (mW) */,
  "throughput" INTEGER /* Throughput (bytes) */,
  "avg_power" REAL /* Average power (mW) */,
  "avg_power_rolled" REAL /* Rolled average power (mW) */,
  "avg_power_baseline" REAL /* Baseline average power (mW) */,
  "network_tower_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network tower involved in the data entry */,
  "signal_latency" REAL /* Latency of the signal */,
  "battery_level" REAL /* Battery level of the device */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage of the device */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_data_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES network_sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., smartphone, tablet) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "device_os" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  "device_os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "device_os_version_date" TEXT /* Date of the last OS update */,
  "device_firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the device */,
  "device_manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the provider */,
  "support_phone" TEXT /* Support phone number for the provider */,
  "provider_website" TEXT /* Website of the provider */,
  "provider_logo_url" TEXT /* URL of the provider's logo */,
  "provider_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "radio_types" (
  "radio_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each radio type */,
  "radio_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the radio type (e.g., NSA+LTE, 5G) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the radio type */,
  "radio_type_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the radio type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("radio_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, tech) */,
  "user_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "user_role_description" TEXT /* Description of the user's role */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network session being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "user_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser, device) used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES network_sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry where the anomaly was detected */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of the anomaly (e.g., low throughput, high latency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "detected_date" TEXT /* Date when the anomaly was detected */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  "anomaly_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the anomaly (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the anomaly was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES device_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "generated_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, reviewed, published) */,
  "report_generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_report_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("report_generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of the location (e.g., city, rural, suburban) */,
  "location_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)
